title: AI Cloud TCO Model
gist: A product description for the SemiAnalysis AI Cloud Total Cost of Ownership Model, an institutional tool covering the ownership economics of AI clouds that buy accelerators and resell GPU compute. It is a methodology/marketing page enumerating what the model analyzes, with no specific numeric forecasts disclosed.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis models AI cloud economics as the business of buying accelerators and reselling bare metal or cloud GPU compute, treating accelerator residual value as a core driver of the investment case.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: Residual value of GPUs is a swing variable in AI-cloud returns; depreciation assumptions on aging accelerators can make or break the business case for neoclouds and their debt investors.
insights.quote: It can be used to evaluate the business case for establishing and running an AI Cloud for various stakeholders from AI Cloud management teams to equity and debt investors, examining the economics of business operations as well as AI Accelerator residual value.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis computes GPU total cost of ownership in dollars-per-hour from upfront server capex, system power consumption, colocation and electricity costs, and cost of capital — not just hardware price.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: GPU rental price floors are set by all-in $/hr TCO including power and financing — a benchmark for whether market rental rates are sustainable or below cost.
insights.quote: GPU Total Cost of Ownership analysis, calculating comprehensive cost of operating GPU servers ($/hr) based on upfront server capex, system power consumption, colocation and electricity costs, costs of capital.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis projects future AI compute cost curves across Nvidia, AMD, Intel, and custom accelerators, covering inference cost per million tokens and training cost per FLOP by accelerator.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: Per-token and per-FLOP cost comparisons across vendors are the key metric for judging which accelerators win on TCO — watch for AMD/custom silicon closing the gap on Nvidia.
insights.quote: Inference throughput, Training throughput, GPU TDP, All-in TDP per GPU, cost of ownership ($/hr), Inference Cost per M tokens, Training Cost per FLOP by accelerator including Nvidia, AMD, Intel, and custom accelerators.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis models how parallelism schemes — Pipeline, Tensor, Expert, and Data Parallel — change GPU inference and training throughput, treating software optimization as a lever on effective compute cost.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: Effective cost per token falls with optimization, not just new hardware; parallelism gains can compress rental margins even without a chip refresh.
insights.quote: Analysis of impact of various optimizations and parallelism schemes (Pipeline Parallel, Tensor Parallel, Expert Parallel, Data Parallel) on GPU inference and training throughput.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis maintains GPU install-base projections by major vendor through 2028 and estimates total GPU unit shipments by vendor through 2034.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: Long-dated (to 2034) shipment forecasts by vendor frame the supply side of the GPU supply-demand balance that drives rental pricing.
insights.quote: Detailed install base by GPU projections through 2028, estimated GPU total unit shipments by major vendor through 2034.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis builds a full three-statement AI cloud financial model supporting variable capital structures, debt/equity mix, cash-vs-PIK interest, and customer prepay assumptions, framing AI clouds as financeable infrastructure plays.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: Capital structure (PIK, prepay, lease terms) materially drives equity IRR in AI clouds — relevant for assessing the debt-heavy neocloud financing wave.
insights.quote: Three statement financial model – Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash flow, including all key balance sheet items – server depreciation, unearned/prepaid revenue, borrowings and more.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis estimates LLM training and inference economics and infers inference-company profitability, positioning the model to judge whether AI inference businesses actually make money.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: Inference-company profitability is an open question; a model that estimates it directly is a screen for which AI app/infra businesses are economically viable versus subsidized.
insights.quote: LLM training and inference economics analysis, pricing trends and inference company profitability estimates.

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis sells the AI Cloud TCO Model as a separate institutional offering excluded from the standard newsletter membership, with quarterly updates and methodology calls.
insights.speaker: SemiAnalysis
insights.so_what: Indicates a premium data-product tier above the newsletter; relevant only as context on SemiAnalysis's monetization, not a market thesis.
insights.quote: These models are not included with the annual membership to the SemiAnalysis Newsletter. All models are separate institutional offerings.
